**COMP0067 App Engineering**

**March 13, 2026**

Department of Computer Science

University College London

## **1\. System Design**

## **1.1 System Architecture Diagram**

![][image2]

### **1.2 Architecture Description**

The Talent Platform is structured as a full-stack Next.js 16 monorepo application utilising the App Router paradigm. The architecture is divided into three primary layers that ensure a clear separation of concerns, from the user interface down to data persistence:

* **Client/Presentation Layer (Browser):** The frontend is built with React components, leveraging the Mantis dashboard template which relies on Material UI (MUI v5) for standardised, accessible UI elements, alongside Tailwind CSS for utility-based styling. User sessions and authentication states are managed client-side via NextAuth.js JWT cookies.  
* **Application/Server Layer (Node.js/Next.js Server):** This middle tier handles routing, security, and business logic. It employs Next.js Middleware for initial authentication and role-based access control (RBAC) gating before page rendering. RESTful API Routes (`src/app/api/`) handle client requests, which are then processed by a dedicated Service Layer (`src/lib/services/`). This Service Layer centralises all business logic (e.g., consent management, recruiter search) ensuring that frontend pages remain strictly as consumers of data.  
* **Data/Persistence Layer:** The application uses a PostgreSQL relational database to store structured data (such as student profiles, consent settings, and job postings). Database interactions and schema migrations are managed through the Prisma ORM, which auto-generates TypeScript types to ensure strict end-to-end type safety between the database and the application logic. The entire stack is designed to be deployed on UCL IXN servers to maintain GDPR compliance and data residency.

## **2\. First Prototype**

## **2.1 Application URL / IP Address**

[https://talentplatform.westeurope.cloudapp.azure.com](https://talentplatform.westeurope.cloudapp.azure.com)

### **2.2 Login Credentials**

**Admin:** admin@ucl.ac.uk / password123

**Student:** student1@ucl.ac.uk / password123, student2@ucl.ac.uk / password123, student3@ucl.ac.uk / password123

**Recruiter:** recruiter.active@example.com / password123, recruiter.pending@example.com / password123

## **3\. Achievement Table**

## **3.1 MoSCoW Requirements Implementation**

| Req ID | Requirement Description | MoSCoW | GitHub Issue & Layer | Current Status |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| N/A | Foundation Lock: Extend Prisma schema, populate seed users, establish .env contracts, and integrate Mantis UI framework. | Must Have | \#13, \#14, \#15, \#47 (L1) | Completed / Merged (Seed users & env setup closed; schema extended). |
| FR-S-1 | Authentication: Route users (Students, Recruiters, Admins) correctly post-login based on roles. | Must Have | \#16 (L1) | In Progress (Code Merged) \- Login routing changes successfully merged. |
| FR-S-6 | Student Profile: Add profile fields (skills, work experience, education degree, projects) for filtering. | Must Have | \#26 (L2) | In Progress (Code Merged) \- Backend upsert operations & UI forms delivered. |
| FR-S-11 | External Links: Allow students to link external profiles (LinkedIn, GitHub, etc.). | Must Have | \#26 (L2) | In Progress (Code Merged) \- Sidebar display and URL fields delivered. |
| FR-R-14 | Job Board: Job posting specifications and student browse view. | Must Have | \#28 (L2) | In Progress (Code Merged) \- Student browse side and dashboard view delivered. |
| FR-A-1 | Admin Approval: Super admin can approve/reject company registrations to maintain platform integrity. | Must Have | \#27 (L2) | In Progress (Code Merged) \- Partner management page and approval flow UI built. |
| FR-A-2 | User Management: Super admin can suspend or ban users violating terms. | Should Have | \#33 (L3) | In Progress (Code Merged) \- Suspend/ban modal, history panel, and UI structure built. |
| FR-S-4 FR-S-5 | CV Management: Upload, update, delete, or replace CVs in PDF/DOCX format. | Must Have | \#29, \#30 (L3) | In Progress (UI Shell Only) \- CV library listing view and upload interface built; pending backend storage decision. |

## **4\. Project Management**

## **4.1 Project Schedule Status**

The project is currently on schedule overall. The student and admin UI components are largely complete, while backend integration is ongoing and company-related pages remain under development.

## **4.2 Issues Requiring Partner Assistance**

At present, the team has not identified any major issues that require specific assistance from project partners.

## **4.3 Team Contributions**

**Chengyu Yang:** Worked on the UI development for the admin section and contributed to writing Report 4\.

**Sadhana Jayakumar:** Deployed the cloud environment and worked on the UI development for the student section.

**Lienqi Peng:** Focused on resolving issues during the initial coding stage and contributed to editing Report 4\.

**Gian Rajaram:** Worked on issue documentation and schema design.

**Adam Benmohamed:** Reviewed the report and supported UI integration.

## **4.4 Partner Meetings**

**Meeting held on 12 March 2026**  
The team met with the supervisor to report implementation progress, confirm deployment of the current prototype through Microsoft Azure for the report submission, and discuss the next review stage, including supervisor feedback in the following week and a possible wider cross-team demonstration in April.

**Meeting held on 5 March 2026**  
The team met with the supervisor to review the project setup and agreed to continue development in a cloned repository, postpone full UCL SSO integration until later in the project, keep building on top of the scaffold platform while allowing UI redesign, and follow up on deployment, storage, and cross-team coordination plans.

## **4.5 Plan for the Next Two Weeks**

In the next two weeks, the team will focus on further refining the backend and database integration for the student and admin features. Although the backend and database have already been implemented for testing purposes, the current dataset is still limited. In addition, the team will continue developing the company-related pages, expand the test data, improve frontend-backend integration, and test core workflows across the system.Further details of the planned work can be found in the team’s GitHub issue tracker. [https://github.com/gianpremrajaram/talent\_platform\_v1/issues](https://github.com/gianpremrajaram/talent_platform_v1/issues)
